ogTitle: Lacmalac vs Lade Vale: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lacmalac and Lade Vale.
heading: Lacmalac vs Lade Vale — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lacmalac, New South Wales and Lade Vale, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lade Vale has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Lacmalac (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lacmalac or Lade Vale safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lacmalac has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Lade Vale scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lacmalac scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Lade Vale scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lacmalac or Lade Vale?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lade Vale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
